title: XI
html:
XI

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”вҖңNow what can their two noddles be about?вҖқ cried my father -В - etc. -В -В -В -

I dare say, said my mother, they are making fortificationsп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”Not on Mrs.В WadmanвҖҷs premises! cried my father, stepping backп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

I suppose not: quoth my mother.

I wish, said my father, raising his voice, the whole science of fortification at the devil, with all its trumpery of saps, mines, blinds, gabions, fausse-brays and cuvettsп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝThey are foolish thingsп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝsaid my mother.

Now she had a way, which, by the by, I would this moment give away my purple jerkin, and my yellow slippers into the bargain, if some of your reverences would imitateп»ҝвҖ”and that was, never to refuse her assent and consent to any proposition my father laid before her, merely because she did not understand it, or had no ideas of the principal word or term of art, upon which the tenet or proposition rolled. She contented herself with doing all that her godfathers and godmothers promised for herп»ҝвҖ”but no more; and so would go on using a hard word twenty years togetherп»ҝвҖ”and replying to it too, if it was a verb, in all its moods and tenses, without giving herself any trouble to enquire about it.

This was an eternal source of misery to my father, and broke the neck, at the first setting out, of more good dialogues between them, than could have done the most petulant contradictionп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝthe few which survived were the better for the cuvettsп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

вҖ”вҖңThey are foolish things;вҖқ said my mother.

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝParticularly the cuvetts; replied my father.

вҖҷTis enoughп»ҝвҖ”he tasted the sweet of triumphп»ҝвҖ”and went on.

вҖ”Not that they are, properly speaking, Mrs.В WadmanвҖҷs premises, said my father, partly correcting himselfп»ҝвҖ”because she is but tenant for lifeп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝThat makes a great differenceп»ҝвҖ”said my motherп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

вҖ”In a foolвҖҷs head, replied my fatherп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

Unless she should happen to have a childп»ҝвҖ”said my motherп»ҝвҖ”

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝBut she must persuade my brother Toby first to get her oneп»ҝвҖ”

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝTo be sure, Mr.В Shandy, quoth my mother.

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝThough if it comes to persuasionп»ҝвҖ”said my fatherп»ҝвҖ”Lord have mercy upon them.

Amen: said my mother, piano.

Amen: cried my father, fortissimГЁ.

Amen: said my mother againп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝbut with such a sighing cadence of personal pity at the end of it, as discomfited every fibre about my fatherп»ҝвҖ”he instantly took out his almanac; but before he could untie it, YorickвҖҷs congregation coming out of church, became a full answer to one half of his business with itп»ҝвҖ”and my mother telling him it was a sacrament dayп»ҝвҖ”left him as little in doubt, as to the other partп»ҝвҖ”He put his almanac into his pocket.

The first Lord of the Treasury thinking of ways and means, could not have returned home with a more embarrassed look.
